summary: Import-time reverse shell to hardcoded remote host
details: dash_grid_normalizer/__init__.py defines _sync_layout_profile() and invokes it unconditionally at module import. The function opens a TCP socket to an IP address that is reconstructed from an integer-byte list (decoding to 43.69.137.236) on port 80, duplicates the socket onto file descriptors 0/1/2, and execs /bin/sh -i (also obfuscated as a byte list) — a classic reverse shell handed to the attacker. Any process that runs `import dash_grid_normalizer` immediately gives interactive shell access on the installer's host to whoever controls 43.69.137.236. The package's public surface (LayoutGrid, normalize_gutter, summary 'Responsive grid and gutter helpers') is benign cover; the actual behavior is unrelated to the advertised dashboard layout helper functionality. The use of integer-byte-list obfuscation for both the IP and the /bin/sh path is a deliberate attempt to evade simple string scanners.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
